D10 Imaging L4 Analyze Reference

Pipeline Order: WB · Demosaic · Matrix · Gamma · 8-bit

the same five pipeline stages, in two different orders, produce visibly different JPEGs from the same RAW

A JPEG out of a camera is not unique — it is the cumulative result of five non-commuting stages: white-balance gain, demosaic, color matrix, gamma encoding, and 8-bit quantization. Swap any two and you get a different image, because most pairs of stages do not commute (linear scaling commutes with linear matrices, but gamma does not).

A test patch is processed twice: Order A = WB → demosaic → matrix → gamma → quantize (the canonical RAW path). Order B = demosaic → gamma → matrix → WB → quantize (gamma before the colour matrix; matrix on non-linear data). Read the trace and watch the final RGB diverge.
